questions.stem: An agent needs to calculate a fee by calling a sub-agent, wait for the result, and then present the fee breakdown to the user. The user should not be aware that a separate agent performed the calculation. Which orchestration pattern is correct?

options.text: HANDOFF with RETURN: true, since the parent agent needs the result back
DELEGATE, because it is a transparent call-and-return pattern where the user does not see the sub-agent
FAN_OUT, because it executes the sub-agent in parallel with the parent

questions.explanation: DELEGATE is the correct pattern for transparent sub-task execution. It invokes a sub-agent synchronously, waits for the result, and maps the returned values back to the parent agent's session variables. The user does not interact with the delegate agent. HANDOFF would transfer the conversation visibly to the target agent.

questions.stem: In ABL's session hierarchy, each agent activation within a session is called a ________.
questions.explanation: A session is the top-level container for an entire conversation. Each agent activation within a session creates a thread. Handoffs create new threads, and at any given time one thread is active and processing messages. Data flows forward through threads via handoff context and session metadata.

questions.stem: A travel planning supervisor needs to search for flights, hotels, and activities simultaneously when the user says 'plan a trip.' All three searches are independent. According to the orchestration guide, which approach achieves parallel execution?

options.text: Define three sequential HANDOFF rules, each with RETURN: true, that execute one after another
Define three HANDOFF rules with overlapping WHEN conditions (all matching 'plan_trip') and RETURN: true, so all three trigger simultaneously
Use a single DELEGATE with all three agent names listed in the AGENT field

questions.explanation: When multiple HANDOFF rules match the same intent (e.g., intent.category == 'plan_trip'), all matching rules trigger simultaneously. Each agent runs independently and returns results to the supervisor via RETURN: true with ON_RETURN handlers that merge the results. Alternatively, multiple DELEGATE entries with overlapping WHEN conditions can also execute in parallel.

questions.stem: The platform includes a built-in CCaaS adapter for live agent transfers when ESCALATE fires. Which CCaaS provider is currently supported?
questions.explanation: Five9 is the first CCaaS integration available in the agent-transfer subsystem. The Five9 adapter manages the full transfer lifecycle: authenticating with Five9, checking agent availability, creating a conversation with full context, and routing inbound agent messages back to the platform session.

questions.stem: An ESCALATE block defines `on_human_complete` with three conditions: `IF human.resolved == true: COMPLETE`, `IF human.needs_agent == true: HANDOFF to specified_agent`, and `IF human.approved_refund == true: CONTINUE`. What happens when the human agent marks the task as 'needs_agent'?

options.text: The conversation ends and a satisfaction survey is sent
The session resumes with the current agent continuing where it left off
The session hands off to the specified agent for further AI-assisted processing

questions.explanation: The on_human_complete block defines post-resolution actions. When the human's action is needs_agent, the HANDOFF action transfers the conversation to another AI agent. COMPLETE would end the conversation, and CONTINUE would resume the current agent's processing.
